related_files.reason_to_check: If class merging behavior changes (e.g., different deduping), Dialog styling may be affected; also check if utility handles undefined/class arrays.

related_files.relationship: Underlying primitive implementation this file wraps.
related_files.reason_to_check: If Radix API changes in a dependency update (prop names, data- attributes, or ref types), this wrapper may need adjustments.

related_files.relationship: DialogFooter typically hosts action buttons — button styling and API changes can affect dialog layout and interactions.
related_files.reason_to_check: If button sizes/margins change, dialog spacing and responsive ordering may need updates.

components/ui/drawer.tsx.short_description: A styled wrapper around vaul's Drawer primitives providing a bottom-sheet Drawer UI for the app.
components/ui/drawer.tsx.description:
This file defines a small, focused UI component library around the vaul Drawer primitives to provide a reusable bottom-sheet style drawer (modal) used by the application. It exists to centralize styling, composition, and types for the Drawer so application code can import a consistent API (Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerTrigger, DrawerOverlay, etc.) without dealing with low-level vaul primitives or repeating tailwind classes throughout the app. The module is a client component ("use client") because it depends on browser-only behavior (portals, focus management) provided by vaul and React refs.

Design decisions: The file exposes the same primitive pieces from vaul but wraps Overlay, Content, Title, and Description with styled React.forwardRef components and small markup (a draggable/handle element inside Content and a default overlay class). Drawer (the Root) is wrapped to provide a default shouldScaleBackground prop. This keeps downstream usage concise (import the named exports and compose them). The code delegates core accessibility, focus trapping, and portal behavior to vaul while handling application-appropriate default styling and layout (e.g., bottom positioning, rounded top corners, muted handle bar, z-index, background opacity).

How it fits: This is a UI primitives module intended to be used anywhere the app needs a drawer/slide-up modal. It relies on a utility cn from '@/lib/utils' for className merging and on Tailwind classes (so it ties into the app's Tailwind config). It should be edited when visual design changes for drawers or when adding new standardized behaviors (e.g., animation, close-on-escape custom handling).

key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a single place to standardize default behaviour for all Drawers (shouldScaleBackground=true) and preserves the underlying vaul API so callers can still supply props like open, onOpenChange, etc.

key_constructs.reasoning: Exposes the primitive trigger so components can attach opening behavior without importing vaul directly, keeping the app-level API consistent.

key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes portal usage and keeps the app code using the same primitives as this module.

key_constructs.reasoning: Provides an explicit close primitive consistent with other components; re-exporting avoids consumers needing to import vaul directly.

key_constructs.purpose: ForwardRef wrapper around DrawerPrimitive.Overlay that applies default full-screen overlay styling (fixed inset-0, z-index, background opacity) and accepts additional classes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures consistent overlay styling for all Drawers and allows callers to add/override classes when needed.

key_constructs.name: DrawerContent
key_constructs.purpose: ForwardRef wrapper that composes DrawerPortal, DrawerOverlay and DrawerPrimitive.Content to render the bottom-sheet drawer body with default layout and a handle element.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates the common Drawer composition (portal + overlay + content + handle) and the app-specific layout (rounded top, margins, default padding).

key_constructs.name: DrawerHeader
key_constructs.purpose: Simple styled container (div) for Drawer header content with responsive text alignment and padding.
key_constructs.reasoning: Standardizes header layout in drawers so pages can use the same spacing and alignment everywhere.

key_constructs.purpose: Styled container intended for drawer actions placed at the bottom using mt-auto to stick to the footer.
key_constructs.reasoning: Gives a single place to guarantee footer layout and spacing; mt-auto makes the footer push to the bottom within a flex column Content.

key_constructs.name: DrawerTitle
key_constructs.purpose: ForwardRef wrapper around DrawerPrimitive.Title to apply consistent title typography classes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Keeps title styles consistent and lets consumers use the primitive Title prop interface.

key_constructs.name: DrawerDescription
key_constructs.purpose: ForwardRef wrapper around DrawerPrimitive.Description to apply muted description styles.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides consistent styling for description text used under the title in a drawer.

forwards refs and props to vaul primitives
provides consistent default styling and layout for drawers

components/ui/hover-card.tsx.short_description: Client-side React wrapper around Radix HoverCard with preset styling and API conveniences
components/ui/hover-card.tsx.description:
This file defines a small UI component module that wraps Radix UI's HoverCard primitives to provide a project-consistent hover card (popover) component. It exists to centralize default styles, className merging, and prop defaults (align, sideOffset) so that consuming code can import a simple, styled HoverCard, HoverCardTrigger, and HoverCardContent without repeating the same configuration. It also forwards refs for the content element so it plays nicely with other React patterns and libraries.

The design is intentionally minimal: HoverCard and HoverCardTrigger are re-exported directly from @radix-ui/react-hover-card to preserve API parity, and HoverCardContent is wrapped with React.forwardRef to attach default classes (animations, sizing, background, border, etc.) via a cn() utility. This keeps markup concise for consumers and ensures consistent animations and theme token usage (bg-popover, text-popover-foreground). The component is marked "use client" so it runs on the client in Next.js/React Server Component environments.

This file fits into the larger UI system as a building block used anywhere a hover-triggered information card is required (tooltips with richer content, preview cards). It relies on two external concerns: Radix for behavior/accessibility primitives and the project's styling system (Tailwind/utility classes and the cn utility). There are no unit tests provided in the group; behavior is likely validated through integration/storybook/visual testing in the larger app.

key_constructs.name: HoverCard
key_constructs.purpose: Re-export of HoverCardPrimitive.Root to act as the root component for hover card usage
key_constructs.reasoning: Keeping a local alias ensures consumers import from the app's component library rather than directly from Radix, which simplifies future swaps or additional local behavior.

key_constructs.name: HoverCardTrigger
key_constructs.purpose: Re-export of HoverCardPrimitive.Trigger used to mark the interactive element that opens the card
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposes Radix's trigger component through the project's component layer for consistent import paths and potential future extension.

key_constructs.name: HoverCardContent
key_constructs.purpose: ForwardRef-wrapped component around HoverCardPrimitive.Content that applies default alignment, offsets, and className styling
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes default UI styles (size, border, background, animations) and prop defaults so all hover-card content is visually consistent and compatible with accessibility primitives.

key_behaviors: renders a hover-triggered popover
applies default animations and styling to popover content
forwards refs to the underlying Radix content element

pitfalls.consequence: displayName may become undefined, which can make debugging/DevTools less useful
pitfalls.prevention: Use a fallback string when assigning displayName, e.g., HoverCardContent.displayName = HoverCardPrimitive.Content.displayName || 'HoverCardContent'

components/ui/form.tsx.short_description: React form primitives that bridge react-hook-form state to UI components with ARIA-friendly labels, descriptions and messages.
components/ui/form.tsx.description:
This file provides a small library of React UI primitives designed to integrate react-hook-form with accessible form UI elements used across the app. It exists to centralize patterns for wiring field state (errors, touched, etc.) into label, control, description and message components, so the rest of the UI code can consistently display validation state and ARIA attributes without repeating the same plumbing.

Design-wise, it exposes a Form alias (FormProvider from react-hook-form) and a FormField wrapper around react-hook-form's Controller which populates a context containing the field name. A parallel FormItem context provides a unique id for a logical form item. The hook useFormField consumes both contexts and react-hook-form's useFormContext to return ids and field state that child components (FormLabel, FormControl, FormDescription, FormMessage) use to set htmlFor, id, aria-describedby and aria-invalid attributes. This creates a consistent accessible mapping between a label, control and any supplementary text or error messages.

The file is typed with generics to stay compatible with react-hook-form FieldValues and FieldPath types, and uses Radix primitives (LabelPrimitive, Slot) plus local Label component and utility cn function. It's intended to be used in client components (note the "use client" directive) and to be dropped into form UIs across the app so form field markup and accessibility is handled uniformly.

key_constructs.name: Form
key_constructs.purpose: Alias for react-hook-form's FormProvider so the rest of the code can import a local Form component.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a single import point and semantic name for wrapping forms with react-hook-form's context. Using an alias allows the project to swap or extend provider behavior in one place later.

key_constructs.name: FormFieldContext
key_constructs.purpose: React context that holds the active field name for the current FormField.
key_constructs.reasoning: Supplies the field name down the tree without passing props through many components; used by useFormField to query react-hook-form for field state.

key_constructs.name: FormField
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper component around react-hook-form's Controller that provides the field name into FormFieldContext.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates Controller usage and ensures the field name is available to label/control/description/message components so they can derive ids and access field state.

key_constructs.name: useFormField
key_constructs.purpose: Hook that reads FormFieldContext and FormItemContext and uses useFormContext() to return ids and field state (error, touched, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes logic for deriving consistent id values and extracting the current field state. Consumers (FormLabel, FormControl, etc.) use this hook so they don't each have to call useFormContext and compute ids separately.

key_constructs.name: FormItemContext
key_constructs.purpose: React context used to provide a generated id for the current FormItem.
key_constructs.reasoning: Allows multiple child components to anchor on the same unique id for attributes such as id and aria-describedby to keep relationships consistent across the item.

key_constructs.name: FormItem
key_constructs.purpose: A container component that generates a unique id via React.useId and provides it through FormItemContext, while also supplying layout spacing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Groups related label/control/description/message into a single logical unit and ensures unique ids for each instance; the use of useId avoids manual id generation.

key_constructs.name: FormLabel
key_constructs.purpose: Label component that uses field state to apply an error style and ties the label to the control via htmlFor.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a consistent label implementation that reacts to validation state and uses the computed formItemId to link to the input element for accessibility.

key_constructs.name: FormControl
key_constructs.purpose: Slot-wrapped control component that sets id, aria-describedby and aria-invalid based on the field state.
key_constructs.reasoning: By using Radix's Slot, any input-like component can be slotted in and will receive the correct aria attributes and id, keeping markup declarative and accessible.

key_constructs.name: FormDescription
key_constructs.purpose: Paragraph component that renders auxiliary descriptive text with the computed formDescriptionId.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes styling and id assignment for optional helper text that should be referenced by aria-describedby.

key_constructs.name: FormMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Paragraph component that renders the validation message (if present) or children otherwise, and hides itself if there is no body.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures error messages are shown consistently and are announced via aria-describedby when present; returns null when there's nothing to display to avoid empty DOM nodes.

key_behaviors: exposes react-hook-form provider as Form
wraps Controller to propagate field name context
computes and provides consistent ids for label/control/description/message
sets aria attributes (aria-describedby, aria-invalid) based on validation state
conditionally renders validation messages

pitfalls.mistake: Call useFormField outside of a FormField provider or FormItem provider.
pitfalls.consequence: useFormField will attempt to read fieldContext.name before verifying the context and this can cause undefined name usage or runtime errors when calling getFieldState(). The current code checks for fieldContext after using it, which can mask the real issue.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure FormField wraps children that use useFormField and reorder checks in useFormField to verify contexts before accessing properties (check fieldContext and itemContext exist before calling getFieldState).

pitfalls.mistake: Modify the id generation approach (e.g. remove React.useId) without considering server/client rendering.
pitfalls.consequence: IDs may mismatch between server and client leading to hydration warnings or broken label/control associations.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep React.useId (supported in 'use client' components) or provide a deterministic id prop fallback that is set server-side when SSR is involved.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume Controller children always render an element that accepts the Slot props.
pitfalls.consequence: Using FormControl with components that do not forward refs or do not accept arbitrary props will break aria/id propagation and accessibility.
pitfalls.prevention: Use components that correctly forward refs and accept props or wrap them to forward necessary attributes; document that slotted controls must forward refs and accept id/aria attributes.

useFormField: main logic that queries react-hook-form and computes ids
FormControl and FormLabel: show how ARIA attributes and error styling are applied
skip_unless_needed: FormDescription and FormMessage: simple presentational components once you understand id composition
Form and FormItemContext: boilerplate context/provider wiring

related_files.reason_to_check: If label styling or ref-forwarding changes, FormLabel behavior and accessibility may need adjustment.

related_files.relationship: Contains cn utility used to compose className conditional strings.
related_files.reason_to_check: If class name composition semantics change, appearance of FormLabel, FormDescription, and FormMessage will be affected.

related_files.relationship: Provides Controller, FormProvider, useFormContext and getFieldState used throughout this file.

components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx.short_description: A set of styled React wrapper components around Radix UI's dropdown primitives for consistent dropdown menus.
components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx.description:
This file provides a collection of React components that wrap @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu primitives and apply a consistent set of styling, class merging, and iconography for the app's dropdown menus. It exists to centralize visual and behavioral conventions (Tailwind classes, animations, spacing, focus states, and icons) so all dropdowns across the app look and behave the same while keeping the markup ergonomics simple for consumers.

The wrappers are thin: they forward refs, combine className with a local set of utility classes (via the cn helper from '@/lib/utils'), and expose the same APIs as Radix primitives (e.g., props and controlled pieces like checked for CheckboxItem). The file also maps displayName values to the underlying Radix primitive displayNames to aid debugging and React DevTools. The implementation favors composition and reusability—exporting Root, Trigger, Content, Item, CheckboxItem, RadioItem, Label, Separator, Sub, SubTrigger, SubContent, Portal, Group and RadioGroup—so callers import only what they need.

Design choices: The file uses 'use client' to ensure these are client-only React components (Radix uses DOM APIs). The heavy use of Tailwind utility classes encodes theming decisions (e.g., bg-popover, data-[state=open] animation hooks) and the wrappers intentionally keep them minimal so callers can pass className to extend styles. Accessibility and keyboard behavior are delegated to Radix; this module's responsibility is consistent UX styling and small composition conveniences (icons, spacing for inset items, ItemIndicator placement).

Because this is a UI primitive layer, changes here are likely to affect many places. Related changes often include theme tokens, the cn utility, and any global Tailwind config or design tokens (e.g., bg-popover, accent classes).

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenu
key_constructs.purpose: Root provider for dropdown menus (alias of Radix's Root).
key_constructs.reasoning: Consumers use this as the top-level container for dropdown state; keeping it exported under the local name establishes a consistent import surface across the app.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuTrigger
key_constructs.purpose: Trigger element that toggles the dropdown (alias of Radix's Trigger).
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed so callers can render the element that opens the menu while benefiting from the file's exports and consistent usage.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuGroup
key_constructs.purpose: Grouping wrapper for related menu items (alias of Radix's Group).
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides semantic grouping for items; exported to allow grouped layouts consistent with Radix semantics.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuPortal
key_constructs.purpose: Portal wrapper for rendering content outside DOM hierarchy (alias of Radix's Portal).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by Content to ensure overlay positioning works and to avoid overflow clipping by parent containers.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuSub
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper representing a submenu root (alias of Radix's Sub).
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposes Radix's sub-menu primitive so the app can create nested menus with the same styling conventions.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuRadioGroup
key_constructs.purpose: Radio group primitive for grouping radio menu items (alias of Radix's RadioGroup).
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed for cases where multiple radio items are needed; maintains the same API as Radix.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuSubTrigger
key_constructs.purpose: Styled wrapper around SubTrigger that adds inset support and an arrow icon.
key_constructs.reasoning: Submenus need a trailing caret and optionally inset spacing; this component centralizes that behavior (adds ChevronRight and pl-8 when inset).

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuSubContent
key_constructs.purpose: Styled wrapper around SubContent to apply consistent sizing, animation, and theming.
key_constructs.reasoning: SubContent should share the same animation and style tokens as Content but in its own wrapper for separation and minor differences.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuContent
key_constructs.purpose: Styled Content wrapper that renders in a Portal and applies animations, border, background, and min width.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the primary visible popup area; exposing it lets callers override sideOffset and className while keeping consistent default presentation.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuItem
key_constructs.purpose: Styled Item wrapper adding selectable behavior, focus styles, and optional inset padding.
key_constructs.reasoning: Items share spacing/focus/disabled styles; providing a single component prevents duplication and mistakes across the app.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuCheckboxItem
key_constructs.purpose: CheckboxItem wrapper with ItemIndicator and absolute positioning for the check icon.
key_constructs.reasoning: Checkbox-style menu items need a consistent indicator placement and icon; this component sets that up so callers only provide children and optional controlled checked prop.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuRadioItem
key_constructs.purpose: RadioItem wrapper with ItemIndicator and absolute positioning for the radio dot.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides consistent radio indicator placement and appearance to match CheckboxItem and other items.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuLabel
key_constructs.purpose: Label wrapper for section headers in menus; supports inset spacing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Labels often need bold font and spacing; this component centralizes that style and optional inset alignment with items.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuSeparator
key_constructs.purpose: Visual separator (hr) between menu sections with consistent margin and background token.
key_constructs.reasoning: Shared separator prevents inconsistent dividers across menus and respects app spacing tokens.

key_constructs.name: DropdownMenuShortcut
key_constructs.purpose: Small helper to render keyboard shortcut text aligned to the right within items.
key_constructs.reasoning: Shortcut text is a repeated pattern (small, right-aligned); a tiny component keeps item bodies clean and consistent.

components/ui/input-otp.tsx.short_description: React wrapper components around a third‑party OTP input library providing styled slots, group and separator.
components/ui/input-otp.tsx.description:
This file supplies client-side React components that wrap and style an OTP input implementation from the external "input-otp" package for use in the app's UI. It exists to centralize styling, accessibility roles, and composition helpers (group, slot, separator) so pages can easily render a consistent OTP input across the app. The file is a Next.js client component ("use client") so it can use hooks and context from the OTP input package on the client.

Design-wise it exposes four small composable components: InputOTP (the main controlled OTPInput), InputOTPGroup (a simple flex container for layout), InputOTPSlot (renders a single slot using OTPInputContext with styling and a fake caret), and InputOTPSeparator (renders a visual separator using lucide-react's Dot). The components use forwardRef to preserve refs, TypeScript's React.ElementRef/ComponentPropsWithoutRef for typed props, and the project's cn utility for conditional Tailwind class composition. InputOTPSlot consumes OTPInputContext.slots[index] to display the character, active state, and to show an animated fake caret when the library indicates it.

This wrapper centralizes patterns and Tailwind classes (focus ring, borders, sizing, fake caret animation) so individual pages don't need to replicate class names. It relies on the host app's Tailwind config (classes like ring-offset-background, animate-caret-blink) and the input-otp library's context shape. Because InputOTPSlot directly accesses context.slots by index, it needs to be rendered inside the provider created by the OTPInput component; otherwise it will throw or behave unexpectedly.

key_constructs.name: InputOTP
key_constructs.purpose: Main wrapper around OTPInput from the input-otp package that applies app-specific class names and forwards refs/props.
key_constructs.reasoning: When integrating a third-party OTP control you typically want to enforce consistent spacing, disabled styles, and ref forwarding for parent components. This wrapper centralizes those concerns so callers only import InputOTP.

key_constructs.name: InputOTPGroup
key_constructs.purpose: Simple wrapper div providing a flex container for arranging slots or separators.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a semantic, reusable grouping component to keep layout consistent and allow callers to pass refs or className without dealing with lower-level markup.

key_constructs.name: InputOTPSlot
key_constructs.purpose: Renders a single visual OTP slot using OTPInputContext.slots[index], shows its character, active ring, and a fake caret when appropriate.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates the slot presentation logic (borders, rounding for first/last slot, fake caret placement) and binds it to the OTPInput context so consumers only supply index and optional className.

key_constructs.name: InputOTPSeparator
key_constructs.purpose: A small component rendering a separator (Dot icon) with role="separator" for accessibility.
key_constructs.reasoning: Separators are a recurring UI pattern between groups of slots; exposing a tiny component lets callers inject a consistent divider while preserving semantic roles.

key_behaviors: renders a styled OTPInput with consistent container and input classes
renders individual OTP slots from OTPInputContext using index
shows an animated fake caret inside the active slot
provides a visual separator between groups of slots

pitfalls.mistake: Render InputOTPSlot without being inside an InputOTP (OTPInput) provider or passing an index out of range.
pitfalls.consequence: OTPInputContext may be undefined or slots[index] may throw, causing runtime errors or rendering of incorrect values.
pitfalls.prevention: Only use InputOTPSlot as a child of InputOTP (or ensure OTPInputContext is available) and ensure the index corresponds to an existing slot.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume Tailwind classes like animate-caret-blink or has-[:disabled] exist by default.
pitfalls.consequence: Styles or animations won't appear; classes such as has-[:disabled] appear custom/experimental and may be unsupported if Tailwind config/plugins differ.
pitfalls.prevention: Verify the project's Tailwind config contains the custom classes/variants and animation utilities, or update styles to match the app's configuration.

pitfalls.mistake: Converting this file to a server component (removing "use client").
pitfalls.consequence: OTPInputContext and other client-only behaviors (refs, hooks, DOM-dependent animations) will break at runtime.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep "use client" at the top; only refactor to server components if replacing client-side OTP logic with a compatible SSR approach.

InputOTPSlot: how it reads OTPInputContext.slots[index], renders char, active ring and fake caret
InputOTPSeparator: small accessible separator component using Dot icon
